title: artemisia ludoviciana var. douglasiana
intro: Artemisia ludoviciana var. douglasiana, also known as Douglas' mugwort, is a perennial herb belonging to the daisy family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to its high concentration of volatile oils and sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Douglas' mugwort include its ability to aid digestion, reduce menstrual cramps, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being.
